turns.content: I'm building a simple CLI tool and I keep trying to handle errors everywhere. Should I just use unwrap() on every Result or is there a better way? My current code looks messy.

Using unwrap() on every Result is definitely not idiomatic; it will panic at the first error and crash your program. Instead, you should use combinators like map, map_err, or_then to chain operations safely without losing context. For example, if you have a function that returns Option<String> representing a file path, you can convert it to Result using ok() so your main logic accepts a Result type consistently.
Ah okay so I should be converting my Option steps into Result? But what if I want to skip an error and continue with the next command? Can I just use unwrap_or_default on everything?

unwrap_or_default is useful for optional values like configuration defaults, but it won't help you chain commands that might fail. If you want to skip an error and continue, you should use or_else instead; this lets you provide a fallback action when the Result contains Err, rather than just unwrapping and ignoring failures.
